_meta.description: Help Base content for NinerLog. Each key maps to a help section ID. Values are markdown rendered via react-markdown. Images written as ![caption](figure:<id>) render theme-aware inline-SVG illustrations from src/pages/help/figures.tsx; the illustration follows the app's light/dark toggle.

getting-started:
# Getting Started with NinerLog

Welcome to **NinerLog** — your digital pilot logbook for EASA, FAA, and other regulatory authorities. This guide takes you from an empty account to your first logged flight in six short steps.

> **Prefer a guided tour?** Click **Replay tour** in the top-right of this Help page to walk through the app interactively at any time.

---

## Find Your Way Around

Everything lives in the **left sidebar** (a bottom bar on mobile). This is your map to every feature — click any item to open it.

![The left sidebar is your map to every feature](figure:nav-overview)

---

## 1. Create Your Account

Visit the registration page and enter your **name**, **email**, and a **strong password** (minimum 8 characters). You'll be logged in immediately after registration. Verify your email when the confirmation message arrives so expiry reminders can reach you.

> **Tip:** We strongly recommend enabling **two-factor authentication (2FA)** or a **passkey** in your profile settings for account security.

---

## 2. Add Your License

Go to **Licenses** from the sidebar. Click **Add License** and enter:

- **Regulatory Authority** — e.g. EASA, FAA, CAA UK
- **License Type** — e.g. PPL, CPL, ATPL, SPL
- **License Number** — your official license number

After creating a license, add your **class ratings** (e.g. SEP Land, MEP Land, IR) with their issue and expiry dates.

![Add a license, then add its class ratings](figure:add-license)

---

## 3. Add Your Aircraft

Go to **Aircraft** and add the planes you fly. For each aircraft, enter:

| Field | Example |
|-------|--------|
| Registration | D-EABC |
| Type Code | C172 |
| Make & Model | Cessna 172SP |
| Aircraft Class | SEP Land |

The aircraft class determines which class ratings your flights count toward.

---

## 4. Log Your First Flight

Go to **Flights** and click the **+ Add Flight** button in the top-right.

![The + Add Flight button sits at the top of the Flights page](figure:add-flight)

Fill in:

1. **Date** — the flight date
2. **Aircraft** — select from your fleet (auto-fills type)
3. **Departure / Arrival** — ICAO codes (e.g. EDDF, EDDH)
4. **Off-block / On-block times** — chocks off and on (total time auto-calculated)
5. **Landings** — day and night landing counts

NinerLog **auto-calculates** solo time, cross-country time, distance, day/night splits, and more based on your input.

---

## 5. Check Your Currency

Visit the **Currency** page to see your status per class rating. NinerLog evaluates:

- ✅ **EASA** revalidation rules (FCL.740.A)
- ✅ **FAA** 14 CFR 61.57 requirements

**Green** means current, **amber** means attention needed, **red** means not current.

![Traffic-light status per rating on the Currency page](figure:currency-status)

---

## 6. Add Your Credentials

Go to **Credentials** to track your:

- Medical certificate (EASA Class 1/2/LAPL, FAA Class 1/2/3)
- Language proficiency (ICAO Level 4/5/6)
- Security clearances

NinerLog will **warn you before they expire** via email notifications.

---

## Make It Yours

Open **Profile & Settings → Preferences** to pick your **theme** (Light, Dark, or System), language, date format, and how times are shown. Every screen — including the illustrations in this Help Base — follows your theme choice instantly.

![Switch between Light, Dark, and System in Preferences](figure:theme-toggle)

---

## You're All Set! 🎉

Explore the **Dashboard** for an at-a-glance summary, the **Reports** page for statistics, the **Map** for route visualization, **Quick Log** for one-tap block timing in the cockpit, and **Import** to bring in flights from ForeFlight or other logbooks.
dashboard:
# Dashboard

The **Dashboard** is your home screen — the first thing you see after signing in. It answers three questions at a glance: *Am I current? How much have I flown? What did I fly recently?*

![The Dashboard: greeting, quick log button, stat tiles, currency, and recent flights](figure:dashboard-overview)

---

## What's on the Dashboard

| Area | What it shows |
|------|---------------|
| **Greeting & Log Flight** | A time-of-day greeting and a **Log Flight** shortcut that jumps straight to a new flight form |
| **Flight currency** | Quick traffic-light chips for passenger, night, and instrument recency so you know your status the moment you land |
| **Credential alerts** | Banners when a medical, rating, or clearance is expiring soon or has expired |
| **Statistics** | Total time, PIC time, total flights, and landings (day / night) |
| **Block time breakdown** | How your hours split across PIC, dual, solo, cross-country, night, and IFR |
| **Time by class** | Hours, flights, and landings grouped by aircraft class |
| **Recent flights** | Your latest entries — click any one to open its details |

---

## Tips

- The **Log Flight** button is the fastest way to start a new entry from anywhere.
- A **signed** badge on a recent flight means an instructor has signed it and it's locked from edits — see **Instructor Signatures**.
- Numbers here reflect any **starting balance** you set for hours carried over from a paper logbook (configure it under **Profile & Settings → Data & Security**).
flights:
# Logging Flights

NinerLog's flight form captures all the data you need for **EASA and FAA logbook compliance**, while auto-calculating everything it can from what you enter.

---

## Creating a Flight

Go to **Flights** and click the **+ Add Flight** button in the top-right.

![Start a new entry from the + Add Flight button](figure:add-flight)

Then fill in the form. The fields that change your logged time are always visible; rarely-needed detail lives in collapsible drawers you can expand when you need them.

![The flight form: always-open sections plus collapsible drawers](figure:flight-form)

---

## Form Sections

| Section | Always visible? | Fields |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| **Basic** | Yes | Date, aircraft, departure → arrival |
| **Route & Times** | Yes | Off/on-block, departure/arrival times, route waypoints |
| **Landings** | Yes | Day/night landings and takeoffs |
| **Crew** | Yes | Crew members with roles (PIC, SIC, Instructor, Student, Passenger, Safety Pilot, Examiner) |
| **Instrument / IFR** | Collapsed | IFR time, actual/simulated instrument time, holds, approaches, IPC |
| **Training & Currency** | Collapsed | Ground training, simulated (FSTD) time, Flight Review, Proficiency Check, multi-pilot time |

---

## Auto-Calculated Fields

NinerLog automatically computes these values from your input:

| Field | How It's Calculated |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Total time** | From off-block to on-block |
| **Night time** | Using sunset/sunrise at your route airports |
| **Solo time** | When no crew members and not a dual flight |
| **Cross-country** | When departure ≠ arrival |
| **Distance** | Great-circle distance between airports |
| **Day/night split** | Landing split based on sunset at arrival airport |
| **PIC / Dual** | Based on whether an instructor is on board |
| **IFR total** | From actual + simulated instrument time |
| **SIC time** | When SIC role is assigned |

---

## Crew — the Single Source of Truth

Add crew members with their roles:

- **PIC** — Pilot in Command
- **SIC** — Second in Command
- **Instructor** — Flight instructor (makes the flight "dual")
- **Student** — Student pilot
- **Passenger** — Non-flying passenger
- **Safety Pilot** — Required for simulated instrument
- **Examiner** — Check ride examiner

NinerLog **saves contacts** for auto-complete on future flights.

> **Single source of truth.** **Crew** is the **only** place where you tell NinerLog who else is in the cockpit. There is no separate "PIC Name" or "Instructor Name" field — those are derived automatically from the crew list and used for EASA logbook columns and exports.

---

## How to Log Common Scenarios

The scenario is determined entirely by **who you list under Crew**:

| Scenario | Who you add | Result |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| **Solo / PIC alone** | _Nobody_ | PIC time = total time, solo time = total time |
| **PIC with passengers** | Each passenger as **Passenger** | PIC time = total time |
| **Two-pilot crew, you are PIC** | Other pilot as **SIC** | You get PIC time |
| **Two-pilot crew, you are SIC** | Other pilot as **PIC** | You get SIC time, the other pilot is PIC of record |
| **Dual received (you are the student)** | Instructor as **Instructor** | You get Dual time, the instructor is PIC of record |
| **Dual given (you are the instructor)** | Student as **Student** | You get PIC time + Dual Given time |
| **Check ride / proficiency check** | Examiner as **Examiner** | Examiner is logged; PIC/Dual derived from your role |
| **Simulated instrument practice** | Safety pilot as **Safety Pilot** | Required by 14 CFR 91.109 for simulated IMC |

> **Tip:** If both you and a third-party instructor are listed as **Instructor**, the third-party takes precedence and the flight is logged as Dual received (you as the student).

---

## Searching & Filtering Flights

The search bar above the flight list understands both **free text** and **structured tags**. Click the **?** button on the right of the search bar to open the tag reference and autocomplete.

![Click the ? button for the tag reference; combine tags with AND / OR / -not](figure:flight-search)

- **Free text** — type any word to match aircraft, airports, remarks, or crew.
- **Tags** — narrow precisely, e.g. `from:EDDF`, `to:EDDH`, `reg:D-E*`, `crew:"John Doe"`, `night>0`, `ifr>0`, `date:2026-05`, `totalTime>1:30`.
- **Booleans** — combine with `AND`, `OR`, parentheses, and a leading `-` to negate, e.g. `night>0 AND (from:EDDF OR to:EDDF)`.
- **Wildcards & quotes** — `reg:D-E*` matches a prefix; quote multi-word values like `crew:"Jane Roe"`.

---

## Editing & Deleting

Click any flight in the list to view details. Use the **edit** button to modify or the **delete** button to remove it.

> ⚠️ Deleted flights **cannot be recovered**. If a flight is locked by an instructor signature, void the signature before editing — see **Instructor Signatures**.
quicklog:
# Quick Log

**Quick Log** is a stripped-down, one-tap timer for capturing block times *while you fly* — ideal on your phone in the cockpit. Later you open the entry on the **Flights** page and fill in the remaining detail at your desk.

![Big Off-block / On-block tap targets, built for the cockpit](figure:quicklog)

---

## How It Works

1. Open **Quick Log** from the sidebar (or **More** on mobile).
2. Pick the **aircraft** you're flying — the last one you used is remembered. Not in your fleet yet? Add it inline.
3. Tap **OFF-BLOCK** the moment the chocks come out. NinerLog stamps the time (and a best-effort GPS position, if available).
4. Fly.
5. Tap **ON-BLOCK** when you shut down. The session becomes a draft flight with the block time already filled in.
6. Later, open it on **Flights** to add crew, landings, and any other detail.

---

## Works Offline

Quick Log is designed for places with no signal. Your taps are **queued on the device** and **sync automatically** when you're back online — so a lost connection at a remote strip never costs you a timestamp.

> **Tip:** Because block times are captured live, they're more accurate than times you reconstruct from memory later. Quick Log then hands off to the full flight form for everything else.
aircraft:
# Aircraft Management

The Aircraft section lets you maintain a database of the aircraft you fly. This enables **auto-fill when logging flights** and powers **currency tracking** per class rating.

---

## Adding an Aircraft

1. Go to **Aircraft** from the sidebar
2. Click **Add Aircraft** in the top-right
3. Fill in the details and save

![The Add Aircraft button and your fleet list](figure:add-aircraft)

| Field | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| Registration | Tail number | D-EABC, N12345 |
| Type Code | ICAO type designator | C172, PA28, DR40 |
| Make | Manufacturer | Cessna |
| Model | Model name | 172SP |
| Aircraft Class | Determines currency tracking | SEP Land, MEP Land, TMG |

---

## Quick-Add from the Flight Form

You can also add aircraft **directly from the flight form**. When you type a registration that isn't in your database, you'll see an option to quick-add it inline — no need to leave the flight you're logging.

---

## Editing & Deactivating

- Click any aircraft card's **Edit** link to change its details
- Mark aircraft as **inactive** if you no longer fly them
- Inactive aircraft still appear in historical flights but won't clutter the flight-form dropdown

---

## Aircraft Class

The aircraft class field is critical — it determines which **class ratings** your flights on this aircraft count toward:

| Aircraft Class | Typical Aircraft |
|---------------|------------------|
| SEP Land | C172, PA28, DR400 |
| SEP Sea | C172 on floats |
| MEP Land | PA34, DA42 |
| TMG | SF25, Super Dimona |
| SET Land | TBM 900, PC-12 |
currency:
# Currency & Recency

The Currency page shows your flight recency status per class rating. NinerLog **automatically evaluates rules** based on your license's regulatory authority.

![Each rating shows a green / amber / red status with the reason](figure:currency-status)

---

## Reading the Page

Every class rating gets its own card with a **traffic-light status** and a plain-language reason (e.g. "Revalidation due in 41 days" or "6 approaches needed"). Expand a card to see exactly which requirements are met and which are outstanding.

---

## EASA Rules

| Class Type | Requirement | Reference |
|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **SEP / TMG** | 12h total + 6h PIC + 12 T&L + 1h instructor within 24 months before expiry | FCL.740.A |
| **MEP / SET** | Proficiency check, or 10 route sectors + 1h instructor in last 3 months | FCL.740.A |
| **IR** | 10h IFR flight time within 12 months + proficiency check | FCL.625.A |

---

## FAA Rules

| Requirement | Details | Reference |
|------------|---------|----------|
| **Day passenger currency** | 3 takeoffs & landings in preceding 90 days | 14 CFR 61.57 |
| **Night passenger currency** | 3 full-stop night T&L in 90 days | 14 CFR 61.57 |
| **Instrument currency** | 6 approaches + holding in 6 calendar months | 14 CFR 61.57(c) |
| **Flight review** | Within preceding 24 calendar months | 14 CFR 61.56 |

---

## Status Indicators

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|--------|
| 🟢 **Current** | All requirements met |
| 🟡 **Attention** | Approaching expiry or requirements |
| 🔴 **Not Current** | Expired or requirements not met |

> **Stay ahead of it.** Turn on the matching alerts under **Profile & Settings → Notifications** to get an email before a currency window closes — see **Profile & Settings**.
licenses:
# Licenses & Class Ratings

NinerLog supports **any regulatory authority** worldwide. Licenses hold your basic license information; class ratings track what you're rated to fly.

---

## Understanding the Model

| Concept | What It Is | Expires? |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| **License** | Your PPL, CPL, ATPL, SPL | No — a PPL is permanent |
| **Class Rating** | SEP Land, MEP Land, IR, TMG | Yes — EASA ratings have expiry dates |
| **Flight** | A logged flight entry | Not linked to any specific license |

> **Key insight:** A flight counts toward **all applicable class ratings** across all your licenses. You don't need to assign flights to licenses.

---

## Adding a License and Its Ratings

1. Go to **Licenses** and click **Add License**, then enter the **authority** (e.g. EASA, FAA), **type** (e.g. PPL, CPL), **number**, and **issuing authority**.
2. On the new license card, click **+ Add Rating** and set the **class type**, **issue date**, and **expiry date**.

![Step 1: add the license. Step 2: add each rating on its card](figure:add-license)

NinerLog tracks revalidation requirements based on the license's authority, and the expiry dates you enter here drive the **Currency** page.

---

## Separate Logbooks

For **SPL** (glider) or **ultralight** licenses, enable **"Requires separate logbook"**. This filters the flights view to show only flights on aircraft matching that license's class ratings — keeping regulator-specific logbooks cleanly apart.
credentials:
# Credentials & Medicals

Track your medical certificates, language proficiency, and security clearances. NinerLog sends **email warnings before they expire**.

![Color-coded status badges for every credential](figure:credentials-status)

---

## Supported Credential Types

| Category | Types |
|----------|-------|
| **Medical Certificates** | EASA Class 1, Class 2, LAPL Medical; FAA Class 1, 2, 3 |
| **Language Proficiency** | ICAO Level 4, 5, 6 |
| **Security Clearances** | German ZÜP/ZüBB, and others |

---

## Adding a Credential

1. Go to **Credentials** from the sidebar
2. Click **Add Credential** in the top-right
3. Select the **type**, enter **issue date**, **expiry date**, and **issuing authority**
4. Add optional **notes** (e.g. restriction codes, examiner name)

---

## Expiry Alerts

NinerLog shows color-coded status badges:

| Status | Meaning | Color |
|--------|---------|-------|
| **Valid** | Not expiring soon | 🟢 Green |
| **Expiring Soon** | Within 90 days of expiry | 🟡 Amber |
| **Expired** | Past expiry date | 🔴 Red |

Enable email notifications in **Profile & Settings → Notifications** to receive warnings at **30, 14, and 7 days** before expiry (the exact schedule is yours to choose).
signatures:
# Instructor Signatures

Student and training flights often need an **instructor's signature**. NinerLog lets an instructor sign a flight digitally — locking that entry so it can't be quietly changed afterward.

![Three ways to collect a signature, right on the flight](figure:signature-section)

---

## Requesting a Signature

Open a flight and find the **Instructor Signature** section. You have three options:

| Option | When to use it |
|--------|----------------|
| **Sign now** | The instructor is with you — hand them the device and they draw their signature on the spot |
| **Request via email** | Send the instructor a secure link they can open and sign from their own device |
| **Get shareable link** | Copy the link yourself and send it however you like (e.g. a messaging app) |

For **email** and **link** requests, the section then shows the pending status — who it was sent to, when it **expires**, and buttons to **Resend** or **Cancel** the request.

---

## Signing a Flight

When the instructor opens the link (or takes the device for **Sign now**), they:

1. See a read-only summary of the flight
2. Enter their **name** and optionally a **certificate number**
3. **Draw their signature** on the pad
4. Submit

That's it — no NinerLog account required for the instructor to sign via a link.

---

## What Signing Does

> 🔒 **A signed flight is locked.** Once signed, the entry can't be edited. A **Signed** badge appears on the flight and in lists.

If a genuine correction is needed, use **Void signature** and give a reason. Voiding unlocks the flight for editing and is recorded in the audit trail; a fresh signature is required afterward.
import-export:
# Import & Export

---

## Importing Flights

NinerLog supports importing from:

| Format | Auto-Detected? | Column Mapping |
|--------|---------------|----------------|
| **ForeFlight CSV** | ✅ Yes | Pre-mapped automatically |
| **Generic CSV** | — | Manual mapping required |

### Import Steps

Go to **Import** and follow the guided flow:

![The import flow: upload, detect, map, preview, confirm](figure:import-steps)

1. **Upload** your file (max 10 MB) — drag it onto the drop zone or browse
2. NinerLog **detects the format** and suggests column mappings
3. **Review and adjust** the mappings if needed
4. **Preview** the import — see valid, duplicate, and error rows
5. **Confirm** to import

> **ForeFlight imports** also bring in aircraft data and crew/person information — creating contacts and assigning roles (Instructor, Student, PIC) automatically.

### Duplicate Detection

NinerLog detects duplicates by matching: **date + aircraft + departure + arrival + total time (±0.1h)**, so re-importing the same file won't create doubles.

---

## Exporting Data

Go to **Export** to download your data:

| Format | What's Included |
|--------|----------------|
| **CSV** | All flight fields, spreadsheet-compatible |
| **PDF** | EASA-style logbook format |
| **JSON** | Full data backup (flights, aircraft, licenses, credentials) |

> **Automated backups?** You can also schedule recurring **cloud backups** to your own storage — see **Profile & Settings → Cloud Backups**.
reports:
# Reports & Maps

---

## Flight Reports

The **Reports** page turns your logbook into charts and statistics.

![Choose a time range, then read your hours as charts](figure:reports-overview)

- 📊 **Block hours over time** — stacked area chart by month
- 📈 **Flights per month** — bar chart
- 🛩️ **Hours by aircraft type** — breakdown with progress bars

Select a time range (**6, 12, or 24 months**) using the toggle in the top-right, and export the view as **CSV** or **PDF**.

---

## Flight Map

The **Map** page visualizes your flight routes on an interactive map:

- ✈️ Flight route lines between departure and arrival airports
- 📍 Airport markers with flight counts
- 🌡️ Activity heatmap view
- 📋 Airport statistics table

> **Tip:** Both the charts and the map respect your current **theme** — they redraw in light or dark to match the rest of the app.
profile:
# Profile & Settings

Open **Profile & Settings** from the bottom of the sidebar. Settings are grouped into tabs across the top.

![The settings tabs: Preferences, Account, Notifications, Cloud Backups, Data & Security](figure:profile-tabs)

---

## Preferences

Set your **theme**, **language**, **date format**, **decimal separator**, and how **times** are displayed (HH:MM vs decimal).

### Theme (Light / Dark / System)

Under **Appearance**, choose **Light**, **Dark**, or **System** (follow your device). The whole app switches instantly — including every illustration in this Help Base.

![Appearance switcher: Light, Dark, or System](figure:theme-toggle)

---

## Account

- **Profile info** — update your **name** and **email**
- **Change password** — current password + a new one (minimum 8 characters)
- **Passkeys** — sign in without a password using Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello, or a security key
- **Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)** — TOTP codes from an authenticator app

### Setting Up 2FA

1. Go to **Account → Two-Factor Authentication** and click **Enable 2FA**
2. Scan the QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password)
3. Enter the 6-digit code to verify
4. **Save your backup/recovery codes** somewhere safe

---

## Notifications

Control email alerts with **granular per-category toggles**.

**Credential Alerts** — Medical Expiry, Language Proficiency, Security Clearance, Other Credentials.

**Ratings & Currency Alerts** — Class Rating Expiry, Passenger Currency, Night Currency, Instrument Currency, Flight Review (§61.56), EASA Revalidation progress.

**Settings** — a **Warning Schedule** (which days before expiry to alert: 30, 14, 7, 3, 1), a **Daily Check Time** (UTC hour for the daily check), and a **Notification History** of everything sent.

---

## Cloud Backups

Schedule **recurring, automatic backups** of your full logbook to your own cloud storage destination. Add a destination, and NinerLog runs backups on a schedule — the **Backup Runs** list shows each run's status so you can confirm your data is safe.

---

## Data & Security

Set a **starting balance** for hours carried over from a paper logbook, and manage account-wide data.

### Danger Zone

> ⚠️ These actions are **irreversible**.

| Action | What It Does |
|--------|-------------|
| **Delete All Flights** | Removes all flights and import history |
| **Delete All Data** | Removes all data but keeps your account |
| **Delete Account** | Permanently deletes your account and all data |
admin:
# Admin Console

The Admin Console is available only to the **platform administrator** (designated via the `ADMIN_EMAIL` environment variable). It appears as an extra **Admin** item in the sidebar for that account.

---

## Dashboard

System-wide **aggregate statistics**:
- Total users, flights, aircraft, credentials, imports
- Locked and disabled accounts count
- Flights this month, new users this week
- Cloud backup destinations configured across users

---

## User Management

View all user accounts (**metadata only** — no flight data or credentials visible):

| Action | Description |
|--------|------------|
| **Disable / Enable** | Prevent a user from logging in |
| **Unlock** | Clear brute-force lockout |
| **Reset 2FA** | Disable 2FA for users who lost their authenticator |

> All actions are logged to the **admin audit trail**.

---

## Announcements

Create **banner messages** visible to all users:

| Severity | Color | Use Case |
|----------|-------|----------|
| Info | 🔵 Blue | General information |
| Success | 🟢 Green | Positive suggestions |
| Warning | 🟠 Orange | Maintenance notices |
| Critical | 🔴 Red | Urgent alerts |

Set an optional **auto-expiry time** in hours.

---

## Maintenance

- **Token Cleanup** — delete expired refresh tokens and password reset tokens
- **SMTP Test** — send a test email to verify email configuration

---

## Config

View non-secret **runtime configuration**: Go version, uptime, migration version, airport database size, rate limits, CORS origins, SMTP status.
